growth_records.strain: uncultured (no isolate; metagenomic bin from Sb-mine soil, Lengshuijiang, China)

up.units: mmol/L/h (volumetric; net community, ~10% abiotic via labile Fe(III))

up.method: ion chromatography; decrease 9.97+/-0.01 -> 6.06+/-0.05 mM over 8 d

sec.method: HPLC-AFS; increase to 0.51+/-0.05 mM over 8 d
colorimetric (540 nm); increase to 2.69+/-0.12 mM over 8 d

sec.compound: N2 (15N2, terminal denitrification product, ~3% of N products)

growth_records.cite: Wang, Liying et al. Nitrate-dependent antimony oxidase in an uncultured Symbiobacteriaceae member. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae204. [PMC11521347]
growth_records.snippet: Sb(III) was reduced from 1.03 +/- 0.01 mM ... to 0.46 +/- 0.06 mM on day 8, and concurrently Sb(V) was increased to 0.51 +/- 0.05 mM ... a decrease in nitrate from 9.97 +/- 0.01 to 6.06 +/- 0.05 mM and an increase in nitrite up to 2.69 +/-
growth_records.notes: MIXED-COMMUNITY soil enrichment microcosm (NOT a pure prokaryote culture); functional organism inferred as uncultured Symbiobacteriaceae sp. by 16S + metagenomic binning. Included because substrate consumption and product formation are fully quantified and time-resolved for a prokaryotic redox metabolism. All rates are VOLUMETRIC (per L culture) computed over the 8-day incubation (192 h); no biomass measured -> cannot normalize to mmol/gDW/h. Arithmetic: Sb(III) uptake = (1.03-0.46) mM / 192 h = 0.57/192 = 0.00297 mmol/L/h (=0.0713 mM/day); note ~10% (57.45 uM) of Sb(III) oxidation was ABIOTIC via labile Fe(III), so net microbial slightly lower. Nitrate uptake = (9.97-6.06)/192 = 3.91/192 = 0.02036 mmol/L/h (=0.489 mM/day). Sb(V) secretion = 0.51/192 = 0.00266 mmol/L/h (=0.0638 mM/day). Nitrite secretion = 2.69/192 = 0.01401 mmol/L/h (=0.336 mM/day). N2O ~17% and 15N2 ~3% of NO3--reduced products (Fig 1E), reported as percentages only. Full MSM composition in Supplementary Information -> medium_composition_stated:false. Nitrate consumption is partly by non-Sb electron donors (organic matter, Fe(II)); a significant negative correlation between Sb(III) oxidation and nitrate reduction (P=0.0003) supports coupling.
